sequence_drift.notes: 'context window' was introduced in 14.3 and used freely. All new concepts introduced in 14.9 (token cost model, input/output tokens, TTFT, streaming, prompt caching, model tier selection, cost-latency-quality triangle, exponential backoff, context window management, circuit breaker, rate limits) are properly defined on first use. 'LLM routing' is used as a synonym for model tier selection (a 14.9-introduced concept). 'Jitter' and 'thundering-herd' appear in §8 within an explanation of exponential backoff; both receive inline definitions and neither is a forward reference to 14.10. §11 defers to 14.10 by name only, which is explicitly acceptable.

length_sanity.notes: Estimated word count ~2650 across all sections (excludes YAML front matter). Within the 2500–4500 standard corpus target.

topic_scope.notes: All content is tightly scoped to cost, latency, and reliability of production AI systems. §6 explicitly disclaims implementation depth ('Implementation is a Semester 2 skill'). Observability is limited to production logging concerns (token counts, latency, error rates). No ML training cost, no hardware infrastructure details, no LLM architecture internals found.

findings: All six required terms defined on first use: 'token' (§5.1), 'TTFT' (§5.2), 'context window' (§5.4), 'exponential backoff' (§5.4), 'circuit breaker' (§5.4), 'prompt caching' (§5.3).
No jargon dumps detected — new terms introduced one at a time with immediate plain-language explanation.

Register is plain and tutorial-style throughout. Minor: 'thundering-herd' in §8 is an industry idiom but receives a brief inline definition ('all clients retry on the same schedule'), keeping it accessible.

soft_findings: Word count estimate (~2650) is close to the lower bound of 2500 — if the actual count is marginally below, this would trigger a length-sanity flag. A precise count is recommended before final sign-off.
verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed. Corpus is well-structured, fully cited across all 4 primary sources, introduces all 14.9 concepts with clear definitions, stays within scope, and is written at an accessible register throughout.
